codeReview.summary: The package mostly matches the intended API and typed error model, but the live executor misimplements timeout handling: the configured connection timeout is applied as a whole-process deadline, so legitimate long-running remote commands are killed and reported as ConnectionTimeout after the SSH session has already connected. The timeout test is also too permissive to catch this contract regression.

issues.description: `execFile` is invoked with `timeout: timeoutMs`, and the callback maps any resulting killed/SIGTERM process to `ConnectionTimeout`. That turns the documented connection timeout into a total command-runtime limit. For example, `executeCommand(host, "sleep 20", { timeoutMs: 5000 })` would connect successfully, then be killed after 5 seconds and misreported as a connection timeout instead of allowing the remote command to finish.

The VAL-INFRA-008 integration test accepts either `ConnectionTimeout` or `ConnectionFailed`, so it does not actually enforce the feature's typed-timeout contract. That makes the timeout regression above easy to miss.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill requires tests before implementation and a red test run, but this worker implemented the SSH source files first and only created tests afterward. The handoff still reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, so that signal is currently unreliable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/shuv/repos/shuvdex/.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md` lines 28-30 and 49-51 require tests before implementation and a failing test run. In the transcript skeleton for worker session `2e4e3401-5fff-4747-b723-268b798ebf3a`, the worker says 'Now let me implement the full SSH package...' and creates `packages/ssh/src/errors.ts`, `types.ts`, `live.ts`, and `test.ts` before the later step 'Now write the tests (TDD - tests first, then verify they fail, then fix):'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture notes are stale for current Effect conventions: they still prescribe `Data.TaggedEnum` and list `packages/core -> @effect/schema`, while the codebase now uses `Data.TaggedError` and `Schema` from `effect`. Future backend workers can be misled unless they cross-check the implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/shuv/repos/shuvdex/.factory/library/architecture.md` line 22 says to use `Data.TaggedEnum`, and line 47 lists `packages/core -> @effect/schema`. Current code uses `Data.TaggedError` in `/home/shuv/repos/shuvdex/packages/core/src/errors.ts` line 9 and `Schema` from `effect` in `/home/shuv/repos/shuvdex/packages/core/src/schema.ts` line 4.

summary: Fail: the SSH package API, typed errors, and tracing are mostly in place, but timeout handling is incorrect because the configured connection timeout is enforced as a full command deadline. I also noted a weak timeout test and stale shared guidance around backend-worker procedure and Effect conventions.
